title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
sub: A midnight blast down the Las Vegas Strip: F1’s second-longest lap, built around a ~1.9km flat-out run past the casinos. Cold, low-grip and glittering, it is pure spectacle wrapped around a genuine slipstream battle.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Off the line into the Turn 1 hairpin and the purpose-built opening complex around the pit and paddock, before the track spills onto the city streets.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The showpiece: after a couple of long straights and the Turn 5-7 sequence, the cars run flat-out for ~1.9km down Las Vegas Boulevard past the casinos, one of the longest full-throttle stretches in F1.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Heavy braking off the end of the Strip into the slow Turn 14, then the technical final corners and a chicane back onto the pit straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: The defining variable is temperature: a late-November desert night can drop near freezing, so getting tyres into their window (and keeping brakes hot) is the whole challenge. Graining and cold-tyre lock-ups shape strategy more than thermal wear.
spots: End of the ~1.9km Strip blast + DRS, from ~350 to ~90 km/h. The biggest braking zone and the prime passing spot.
Heavy braking off the pit straight + DRS into the opening hairpin.
Enormous slipstreams down the Boulevard let cars line up a move into Turn 14.
otNote: The near-2km flat-out Strip creates huge slipstreams and a monster braking zone at Turn 14, so overtaking is plentiful. The cold, low-grip surface adds lock-ups and drama on top.
corners: Left hairpin off the pit straight + DRS in the purpose-built section. An opening-lap flashpoint.
Quick left out of the hairpin.
Fast right joining the city streets.
Medium corners onto the first long straight.
Sweeping lefts that set up the run onto the Strip.
Gentle kinks taken flat as the cars accelerate down Las Vegas Boulevard.
Slight kink near the end of the Strip at top speed.
End of the ~1.9km blast + DRS. The lap’s biggest stop and the prime overtaking spot.
Technical final chicane back onto the pit straight.
geo: Sessions run into the early hours so the Strip glows for a global TV audience. The Saturday-night race slot is unique on the calendar.
~1.9km flat-out down Las Vegas Boulevard, the second-longest full-throttle run in F1 after Baku, with speeds around 350 km/h and enormous slipstreams.
A late-November desert night can approach freezing, the coldest track of the year. Switching tyres on and keeping brakes in temperature is the weekend’s defining challenge.
F1’s glitziest venue, wrapping the sport around the casinos and landmarks of Sin City. A $240M permanent pit and paddock anchor the temporary street layout.
